mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals impending recession, causing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate collapsing consumer demand, which directly reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production above 0.5 percent signals robust manufacturing output that supports short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of instances where INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a stagnant federal funds rate creates a negative real yield environment that erodes equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative real yield periods yield positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in greater than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and corporate revenue growth, generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between PAYEMS levels and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.01 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent signals economic contraction that triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 65 percent of quarters where GDP growth is below 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling employment indicates capital substitution and productivity gains that boost corporate margins and drive positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-PAYEMS divergence signal yields a negative information coefficient over rolling 3-year periods.
